gaps.description: The BoR (version 1.0.0-seed) is a scaffold with all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. This means the DIO has no defined scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, no amendment protocol, and no interpretation rules. The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR per MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights, rendering the entire governance stack ungrounded. Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query but is verifying an empty shell.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights; BoR Articles I–VI (all scaffold)

gaps.description: The MSP seed (1.0.0-seed) explicitly states: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' No substantive MSP rules or genome documents have been published. Nomos (#200) evaluates every Action Proposal against an empty MSP rule set at its second compliance gate, meaning the MSP compliance gate is effectively a no-op. This was intended as a bootstrap state but has persisted since 2026-04-10.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Rules — '(none)'; MSP §Genome References — '(none at seed)'; MSP §Purpose — 'MSP + genome compliance gate'

gaps.description: Spine state tracking shows Arbiter, Nomos, Cerberus, and Senate all cycling through ALIVE/DEGRADED ping-pong transitions, indicating they are running as services. However, these organs exist to enforce, interpret, audit, and legislate within a constitutional framework that is entirely undefined (Gap 01). Arbiter verifies the BoR at scope queries but the BoR is vacant. Nomos evaluates APs against an empty MSP. Cerberus has no statutes to audit against. Senate has no constitutional basis for legislation. The governance architecture is structurally deployed but operationally unmoored.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose — 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication'; BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) — undefined; BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol) — undefined; BoR Article VI (Interpretation Rules) — undefined

gaps.description: The MSP §Amendment Protocol defines a chain where BOR_CONFLICT classifications trigger BoR amendment proposals requiring human principal authorization. However, BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol) is undefined, and Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is also undefined. This creates a circular deadlock: the MSP requires a BoR amendment mechanism that the BoR itself does not define, and the human authority whose reservation would authorize amendments has no constitutional articulation. The `human-principal` mailbox referenced in MSP §Amendment Protocol has no constitutionally defined recipient or decision protocol.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Amendment Protocol — 'human principal'; BoR Article IV — '[To be defined by human principal]'; BoR Article V — '[To be defined by human principal]'; MSP §Relationship to the Bill of Rights — 'When a PEM classifies a policy conflict as BOR_CONFLICT, Senate-Legislator produces a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal'

gaps.description: The Hippocampus organ (conversation memory — one of the five Monad legs alongside Radiant, Graphheight, Minder, and Soul) shows `recent_conversations: []` in the world state. This organ was defined in the architectural vision as the conversation memory store for the DIO, yet it has recorded zero conversations. Either the Hippocampus is not receiving conversation data, or its capture pipeline is broken.
gaps.mission_ref: Architectural vision (memory bab3c3b5): 'Monad expanded to 5 legs: Radiant (world), Graphheight (structure), Minder (person), Hippocampus (conversation), Soul (persona)'; MSP §Purpose — organism's operational constitution should cover all active organs

gaps.description: The SafeVault backup organ shows a pattern of failures over the past week: (1) 2026-05-03 — backup hung then killed with zombie processes (~25h old) blocking sqlite3 .backup on spine.db, Layers 2-3 never started; (2) 2026-05-01 — NAS mount failed; (3) 2026-04-30 — script bug with unbound variable ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS array; (4) 2026-04-28/27 — NAS unavailable. NAS mount failures are the most persistent pattern. The 2026-05-03 incident additionally revealed zombie backup processes from the prior day's run that were never cleaned up, indicating a process lifecycle management gap.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose — 'safety rules that Nomos (#200) evaluates'; operational invariants for backup integrity are implicit in the organism's self-preservation function but not yet codified in MSP rules

gaps.description: MSP §Genome References states: 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' The seed lists '(none at seed)'. As of the world state snapshot (2026-05-03, ~23 days post-bootstrap), no genome documents have been published for any of the 25+ active organs tracked in Spine. Each organ operates without MSP-codified operating rules or risk-tier constraints.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References; MSP §Purpose — 'organism's operational constitution' extends to all organs via genome documents

gaps.description: The Radiant stats block shows: `scheduled: disabled`, `last_dream: null`, `health: never_run`, and `watchdog.last_check_at: null`. Despite extensive memory and context data (69 with embeddings, 27 without, 72% coverage), the automated dream cycle that performs context freshness triage and memory consolidation has never been run via the scheduler. Dream cycles have only occurred via manual/interactive sessions (evidenced by dream cycle reports #12-15 from April 11-12 created by `skl-Radiant-dreamer` and `scr-bash-radiant-dreamer-phase1`). The scheduled autonomous mechanism is not operational.
gaps.mission_ref: Architectural vision: dream system is a core Monad function for pattern recognition and memory lifecycle management; MSP §Purpose — operational objectives include self-maintenance functions

gaps.description: The Graph structural section shows a large volume of `enterprise-cv-test-*`, `tenant-a-cv-test-*`, and `tenant-b-cv-test-*` entities with timestamp-based URNs (e.g., `enterprise-cv-test-1776202263-2256`). These appear to be programmatically generated test entities, potentially from a multi-tenant CV test framework. The MSP contains no rules governing entity lifecycle, test data retention, or graph namespace hygiene. Without genome documents or MSP rules, nothing prevents unbounded accumulation of test artifacts in the production Graph.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References — organ-specific operating rules should cover Graph entity lifecycle; MSP §Purpose — operational constraints should prevent resource exhaustion

      "title": "Bill of Rights constitutional vacuum — all six Articles undefined",

Arbiter (#190) has no enforceable perimeter to verify. Nomos (#200) has no constitutional foundation for its scope gate. The entire Nomos dual-gate compliance architecture (Arbiter scope gate + MSP compliance gate) is structurally inoperable at the constitutional layer. Every authorized action is authorized against an empty perimeter — default-permissive in a system designed for constitutional constraint. This is the root strategic gap from which all others derive.",
      "downstream_effects": [
        "MSP cannot translate undefined BoR articles into machine-actionable rules",
        "Nomos scope gate has no BoR perimeter to verify against",
        "Dream cycle has no strategic filter for memory consolidation priorities",
        "Goal dispatch references MSP sections that cannot exist (§routine-operations)"

Recent goals reference 'MSP §routine-operations' which does not exist. Amendment authority is Senate-only via PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout pipeline, but PEMs require Nomos to block an AP against MSP constraints — and there are no MSP constraints to trigger a PEM."
      },
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The second gate of Nomos compliance (MSP + genome compliance) is an empty check — every AP passes because there are no rules to violate. The PEM amendment pipeline is structurally stalled: Nomos cannot issue a PEM for MSP constraint violation when no constraints exist, which means the Senate-Legislator pathway for MSP amendment has no triggering mechanism. The system is locked in default-permissive mode with no内生 (endogenous) path to exit it — the MSP seed was designed to exit default-permissive mode but contains no rules to actually do so.",
      "downstream_effects": [
        "All 10 recently dispatched goals reference a non-existent MSP section (§routine-operations)",
        "Memory consolidation (dream Phase 2) has no mission-aligned relevance scoring framework",
        "Context triage (dream Phase 1) cannot prioritize by strategic relevance when no strategy is defined",
        "Organ genome documents that would extend MSP with operating rules remain uncreated"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:strategic-gap:dream-cycle-degraded-autonomy",
      "title": "Dream cycle autonomous operation disabled — memory and context pipelines not self-sustaining",
      "description": "Radiant stats reveal the dream cycle — the DIO's mechanism for context freshness (Phase 1) and memory consolidation (Phase 2) — is not operating autonomously: scheduled is 'disabled', last_dream is null, health is 'never_run', and the watchdog (with a 36-hour grace period / 1,296,000ms) has never checked. Context blocks are accumulating without triage (only 5 of 16 expiring within 48h, but no Phase 1 running to prune/consolidate them). Memory blocks are growing without consolidation (53 llm-ops memories averaging 31.8 days age, with no Phase 2 running to merge clusters or absorb duplicates). Embedding coverage is at 72% (69 of 96) with 27 memory entries lacking embedding sidecar access — a known limitation noted in Dream Cycle #15 report. The Phase 2 consolidation pathway requires an interactive Claude Code session (not scheduled-task-compatible with MCP tools), creating a structural dependency on human-initiated sessions for the consolidation phase of the dream cycle.",

        "document": "MSP",

The MSP's purpose of providing 'machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules' presupposes an operational memory substrate. Dream cycle health is the mechanism that maintains this substrate. With scheduled=disabled and health=never_run, the operational memory substrate is degrading."

      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without autonomous dream cycles, context freshness degrades — expired blocks accumulate, TTLs are not reduced, and the Radiant context cache grows stale. The Phase 1/Phase 2 split creates a structural fragility: Phase 1 can run via bash autonomous script (scr-bash-radiant-dreamer-phase1.sh), but Phase 2 requires an interactive LLM session for consolidation — the scheduled task environment lacks MCP tool access (merge_memories, find_similar) and embedding sidecar access. This means even if scheduled were enabled, Phase 2 would still fail or require workarounds (direct psql with 'equivalent logic' as noted in cycles 14-15).
